# DFAM RULES  (v5.7: explicit canonical whitelist mirroring Dfam TEClasses.tsv)
# Dfam is itself an input source AND the canonical authority for this dictionary,

# Global fall-through for canonicalization.  The set below is generated from the
# repeatmasker_type + repeatmasker_subtype columns of Dfam's TEClasses.tsv
# (236 distinct labels: 19 bare top-level Classes + 217 Class/Subtype pairs).
# Bare top-level Classes are emitted as Class/Unknown to preserve the */* output
# format (e.g. PLE -> PLE/Unknown), exactly as the rest of the pipeline does.
# A malformed / non-canonical Dfam label (typo, retired name) matches nothing here
# and correctly falls through to the Global safety net.
